title: Moonbeam Moves from Polkadot to Base, Launches AI Agent Platform
summary: Moonbeam is shifting its blockchain from Polkadot to Base and introducing an AI agent framework. GLMR token holders must bridge their assets to Base before July 31, 2026, but no launch date for the AI platform has been announced.
body:
Moonbeam, a blockchain platform, is making a significant shift by moving from the Polkadot network to Base, a layer-2 blockchain built on Ethereum. Alongside this transition, Moonbeam has unveiled plans for an AI agent framework, though no specific launch date has been provided. 

The company has urged GLMR token holders to bridge their tokens from the Polkadot parachain to Base before July 31, 2026. This move is part of Moonbeam's strategy to leverage Base's infrastructure and Ethereum's ecosystem for greater scalability and functionality. 

For everyday users, this transition could mean enhanced capabilities and integration with Ethereum's vast network of decentralized applications (dApps). The AI agent framework, once launched, could offer automated and intelligent interactions within the blockchain, potentially simplifying complex tasks for users. 

Users should watch for official announcements regarding the launch of the AI agent platform and any updates on the bridging process. Ensuring tokens are bridged by the deadline is crucial to avoid any disruptions in accessing Moonbeam's services on the new network.
twitterThread: Moonbeam is leaving Polkadot for Base and launching an AI agent platform. GLMR holders must bridge tokens by July 31, 2026. 🚀
This shift could bring Moonbeam closer to Ethereum's ecosystem, offering better scalability and more dApps. The AI agents might automate tasks, making blockchain interactions smoother.

If you hold GLMR, bridge your tokens soon. Keep an eye out for the AI platform's launch date. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-05-moonbeam-moves-from-polkadot-to-base-launches-ai-agent-platform
